title: Add Classifieds Section to The Daddy TGM

requirements.text: Create a Classifieds page (route: /classifieds) accessible from the top navigation bar alongside the existing five links. The page should be fully styled in the 1970s retro / early 2000s athletic crossover theme consistent with the rest of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'Classifieds' nav link appears in the Navigation component alongside Home, Read Issue, Archive, The Soapbox, and About.
The page is reachable at /classifieds and renders without errors.
The page header reads something like 'THE DADDY TGM CLASSIFIEDS' or 'WANT ADS & ODDITIES' in the retro masthead style.
The page uses the existing retro color palette, newsprint texture, and athletic accent motifs.

requirements.text: Build a static library of at least 30 fake spoof classified ads written in 1970s Mad Magazine / National Lampoon satirical voice with adult humor and cussing allowed (no real-person defamation). Categories should include: FOR SALE (absurd junk), PERSONALS (outrageous lonely-hearts), HELP WANTED (ridiculous job listings), SERVICES OFFERED (quack remedies, con-artist trades), LOST & FOUND, and ANNOUNCEMENTS. Each ad entry should have: a category label, a bold headline, ad body text (1–4 lines), and an optional fake phone number or PO Box.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 30 unique spoof classified ad objects are defined in a classifiedsLibrary data file (e.g., frontend/src/data/classifiedsLibrary.ts).
Each ad has a category, headline, body text, and optional contact info.
Ad text is written in satirical 1970s voice consistent with the existing contentLibrary.ts style.
At least 5 distinct categories are represented.

requirements.text: Render the classifieds on the Classifieds page in a multi-column newspaper-style grid layout. Ads should be grouped by category with bold category headers (e.g., '★ FOR SALE ★', '♥ PERSONALS ♥') as section dividers styled with retro ornamental borders and diagonal stripe accents. Each ad card should have a typewriter-style monospace body, retro border, and subtle aged-paper background.
acceptanceCriteria: Ads are displayed in a 2–3 column grid on desktop, single column on mobile.
Category section headers are visually distinct with retro ornamental styling and athletic diagonal stripe dividers.
Each ad card uses monospace/typewriter font for body text, consistent with the app's retro type treatment.
The layout is responsive — stacks to a single column on small screens.

requirements.text: Add a 'Place Your Own Ad' submission form at the top or bottom of the Classifieds page. Users select a category from a dropdown, enter a headline (max 60 chars) and ad body (max 200 chars), then submit. Submitted ads are stored in localStorage and appear at the top of the relevant category section. Each user-submitted ad should display a fake auto-generated 1970s-style username (reuse the existing soapboxUtils fake username generator) and a submission timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: A submission form with category dropdown, headline field, and body textarea is rendered on the Classifieds page.
Character limits (60 for headline, 200 for body) are enforced with visible counters.
Submitted ads persist in localStorage and survive page refresh.
User-submitted ads appear at the top of their category, labelled with a fake 1970s username and timestamp.
The form is styled consistently with the retro theme (no plain HTML default styling).

constraints: Do not modify any file listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All new components and data files must follow the existing TypeScript conventions in the frontend/src directory.
Reuse existing retro/athletic CSS utility classes from frontend/src/index.css and tailwind.config.js — do not introduce new color or font tokens.
Reuse the fake username generator from frontend/src/utils/soapboxUtils.ts for user-submitted ads.
nonGoals: No backend persistence — all classifieds data (static library and user submissions) lives in the frontend.
No moderation, reporting, or admin tools for submitted ads.
No integration with the daily seeded issue system — the Classifieds page is a standalone section, not part of the 50-page reader.
No images or media uploads in ad submissions.
